content.problemStatement:
In order to expand its reach to the outer universe and ensure universal peace, the MIB has decided to expand its offices.
Namely, it has established its office on NNN most important planets in the universe, with the base station on the planet SSS. Each office has an intergalactic teleporter that connects it to other planet offices. But the teleporters are bound to a planet's policy, so you cannot teleport to any planet you wish.

Due to intergalactic feuds, the planets are divided into KKK different alliances, numbered 111 through KKK. You know for each planet iii, the alliance that it belongs to. In other words, you're given a sequence A1,A2,…,AnA_{1}, A_{2}, \dots, A_{n}A1​,A2​,…,An​, which says that planet iii belongs to alliance AiA_{i}Ai​.

Each planet iii has a policy of teleportation to the planets of other alliances:


Cost to travel from one planet to another is determined by the alliance of the destination planet. More formally, the cost to reach a planet which belongs to alliance jjj from planet iii is represented as Ci,jC_{i, j}Ci,j​.
Intra-alliance travel is free, i.e., if Ai=jA_{i} = jAi​=j, then Ci,j=0C_{i, j} = 0Ci,j​=0.
For a planet iii, there are some forbidden alliances as well. For these alliances, Ci,j=−1C_{i, j} = -1Ci,j​=−1, given that alliance jjj is forbidden to travel from planet iii.
As MIB is bound to follow the intergalactic rules and since they have limited funds (yes, the government is cheap everywhere), they have decided to find the most optimal way to travel to each of the planets from the base station at planet SSS. With all other agents busy with their missions, you are assigned the task to find these minimum-cost paths. This is your first task in MIB, so don't let them down!


Input Format

The first line will consist of three space-separated integers NNN, KKK, and SSS (number of planets, number of alliances, and base planet).
Second-line will consist of NNN space-separated integers representing AiA_{i}Ai​, the alliance corresponding to the ithi^{th}ith planet.
Each of the next NNN lines will consist of KKK space-separated integers, where the jthj^{th}jth integer of the ithi^{th}ith line represents Ci,jC_{i, j}Ci,j​, the cost to travel from planet iii to alliance jjj.


Output Format
Print NNN space separated integers, the ithi^{th}ith integer representing the minimal cost to reach the ithi^{th}ith planet from the base station. For unreachable planets print −1-1−1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
As 444 and 555 belong to the same component, the cost of traveling in-between is 000.


There are multiple shortest paths from 444 to 111. They are:
4→3→1,4→5→3→14 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 1, 4 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 14→3→1,4→5→3→1, 4→14 \rightarrow 14→1, etc. All of these have equal costs, that is 444.


Only path from 444 to 222 is 4→5→24 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 24→5→2, which costs 333 units.


Similarly for node 333, the cost is 444 units.
